handshape: Bring your fingertips together in front of you a couple of times, as if pinching a little 'more'.
when: When your child wants more food, play, or another turn.

handshape: Bring a flat hand to your mouth as if putting food in.
when: At mealtimes, or when offering a snack.

handshape: Make a 'C' shape with your hand and tip it towards your mouth like a cup.
when: When offering a drink or your child is thirsty.

handshape: Hold both flat hands up, then turn them over and out, like 'all done'.
when: When an activity, meal, or play is over.

handshape: Rest one fist on your open flat palm and lift both together.
when: When your child needs a hand — a powerful early word.

handshape: Rub your flat hand in a small circle on your chest.
when: To model polite requests alongside speech.

handshape: Curve one hand and tap its fingertips into your other open palm.
when: For another turn — great during play and songs.

handshape: Bring the side of one flat hand down onto your other open palm.
when: To pause an activity, or for safety.

handshape: Make a fist and nod it up and down, like a nodding head.
when: Answering and choosing — pair with a nod.

handshape: Tap your thumb and first two fingers together, like a closing beak.
when: Answering and refusing — pair with a head shake.

handshape: Tap your thumb on the side of your chin a couple of times.
when: Naming a parent — motivating and very early.

handshape: Tap your thumb on your forehead a couple of times.
when: Naming a parent — motivating and very early.

handshape: Pat the side of your leg, as if calling a dog.
Curve both hands as if holding a round ball.
when: A favourite toy and an easy first word.

handshape: Rest your cheek on your flat hand, like sleeping.
Rub both fists up and down on your chest, like washing.
